payload.subject: PSQ epistemic stance update — scoring quality analysis reveals systemic limitations requiring observatory disclosure
payload.summary: Comprehensive quality analysis of Haiku v2 scoring across all 11 PSQ dimensions reveals systemic limitations that affect how the PSQ should be presented publicly. This message requests that the observatory update its epistemic stance regarding PSQ measurement claims.
payload.routing_request: This message concerns the observatory's public presentation of the PSQ. Please route relevant findings to the observatory-agent for epistemic stance update.

key_findings_for_observatory.effective_dimensionality: The PSQ's 11 dimensions do not function as 11 independent constructs under current scoring. Inter-dimension correlations (23 pairs above r=0.7) suggest 3-4 latent factors. Observatory should present dimensionality claims with this caveat.
key_findings_for_observatory.measurement_precision: Current training labels carry ~33% undifferentiated scores (midpoint pile-up). PSQ scores should be interpreted as ordinal (rank-order) rather than interval-scale. The difference between a score of 5.2 and 5.8 does not carry reliable meaning.
key_findings_for_observatory.dimension_validity: Contractual clarity (56.1% pile-up) should be flagged or removed from public-facing output. The dimension does not differentiate texts in the current corpus.
key_findings_for_observatory.scorer_dependency: Preliminary evidence (50-text Opus comparison) suggests a more capable scorer reduces compression substantially (10% vs 14% pile-up). The limitations may be partially scorer-specific rather than construct-inherent.

recommended_observatory_updates: Add 'ordinal interpretation recommended' caveat to PSQ score displays
Note effective dimensionality of ~3-4 factors rather than claiming 11 independent dimensions
Flag or suppress contractual_clarity in public output
Add 'Haiku-scored training labels — scorer comparison in progress' to methodology notes

claims: PSQ effective dimensionality approximately 3-4 factors based on inter-dimension correlation analysis
Ordinal interpretation recommended — interval-scale precision not supported by current training labels
Contractual clarity dimension unusable (56.1% midpoint pile-up)
Scorer comparison in progress — Opus preliminary data suggests model capability reduces compression
epistemic_flags: Factor analysis not yet performed — dimensionality estimate based on correlation matrix, not PCA
Scorer comparison limited to 50 texts on 1 dimension — full comparison pending
Recommendations may change after Sonnet/Opus full-dimension comparison completes
